author: MambaTrades
content:
Going to call it here as NQ and ES is stuck inside its range now. It's going to be the last day for me as the rest of the year should be low volume with tends to lead to a lot of chop and manipulation.

Take the rest of the year to reflect and create a list of focus on for 2026. This could be overtrading, risk management, sizing, techincals, psychology, etc. Put it on a post it note and focus on that each day instead of making money.

 If you solely focus on following your rules, manage your emotions, and taking great trades, there is no way you shouldn't be profitable in 2026. Good process and psychology is what will make you profitable. 

Have a great rest of the year everyone! Let's crush it for 2026 @everyone

author: MambaTrades
content:
There are 248 trading days left in the year. 

If you missed a trade, feel fomo, tilt, or took a loss - walk away. The market isn’t going anywhere. You don’t need to make money today. 

Focus on building the good habits for 2026. 1-2 high quality trades if presented. Walking away after a loss. No revenge trading. If you missed a trade and hit its targets, walk away. 

Focus on the process and the money will follow @everyone

content: POC Is the midpoint of value so price moves too efficient there which leads to chop/range. I'm working on adding that to my charts since it helps you prevent taking bad setups @here

author: MambaTrades
content:
Price respected the 1hr bearish gap today. Solid short opportunity to the lows. First day I actually liked shorts @everyone 

Set conditions for prices to go higher or lower. Ex if we respect the 1hr gap and don’t get a body close above, we should go lower. 

You can enter near the LH of the 1hr or find a lower timeframe entry below op to the targets below.  Align the higher times with the lower timeframes for the best trade setups

author: TonyMontana
content:
This TSLA trade is always why I preach HOD scale (lock in realized profit) then just let the trade work and slowly scale out.

Ofc this is easier said than done, but it comes from experience.

Today was a higher prob day on TSLA itself after earnings and that’s why I was ok with 2 trades today.

I had a strong thesis just had to let the probability play out.

This is a good trade to review for everyone and ofc I’ll have my review tonight.

@everyone

author: TonyMontana
content:
I know some people are talking about FOMO right now.

Here’s a perspective.

For myself on Friday I was estatic (ofc I hit a $130k trade) biggest of my career.

But guess what?

The stock ran another 11-12% today.

With the trailers I had this was an extra $25-35k if I rolled them into this week.

If I held my full position (NOT SAYING I WOULD AT ALL) it would be a $1mill + swing.

Think about that.

However, I don’t care.

Why?

Our job as traders is NOT to catch the whole move.

It’s to catch the the highest probability chunk.

I was able to do this and document it for you guys.

But no matter if you’re the best trader in the world you will have feelings and you will NOT catch all the moves.

Observe, learn and refine that’s all we can do.

Hope this helps some people from not full porting to chase TSLA gains.

@everyone
